title: After three heatwaves, French people worry: 'Next year will be worse. How will we cope?'
subhead: Across France, people of all ages have realized how vulnerable they are to extreme temperatures, which have stunned the country during these past weeks.

Hungary's new government dismantles Orban-era think tanks
subhead: The pro-European government of new Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar has ordered the closure of the Mathias Corvinus Collegium and the network of institutions that enabled his nationalist predecessor Viktor Orban to spread his ideas in the country and abroad.

title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in London for forthcoming display at British Museum
subhead: The thousand-year-old, 70-meter-long embroidery depicting William the Conqueror's exploits in medieval England has been removed from its crate after its transfer from France. Staff will begin a thorough check of its condition on Monday, and it will be placed in a custom-made display case a few days later.

title: Pogacar wins 'unforgettable' Tour de France 14th stage, extending overall lead
subhead: The four-time winner had already stamped his authority on this Tour in the previous mountain stages, but he again emphasized his superiority with another devastating late attack.

title: How Dakar caught surfing's new wave: 'It has become a path to success and a way of life'
subhead: The Senegalese coast, with its small villages and beaches where Atlantic swells create powerful waves that draw surfers from around the world, has produced a new generation of young champions eager to ride. Every day, they take on the ocean at about 12 spots along this wild, little-known shoreline.

title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhead: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.

title: Who are the people setting France's forests ablaze?
subhead: Nearly nine out of 10 forest fires in France are started by humans. Some do so deliberately, with the perpetrators often difficult to identify.
